title: Is Eva The Most Mean Spirited Anime Of All Time
display_title: Is Eva The Most Mean Spirited Anime Of All Time
wikitext:
[]: Is Eva The Most Mean-Spirited Anime Of All Time?             Quote Post by bogey » Fri Mar 30, 2012 7:11 pm was Tomino right? was eva a series that said its okay to be fucked up and depressed? do you think at its core that eva is a mean-spirited anime?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Fri Mar 30, 2012 7:20 pm That title belongs to Space Warrior Baldios, NGE was just open ended, not depressing.

[]: Re: Is Eva The Most Mean-Spirited Anime Of All Time?             Quote Post by Fireball » Fri Mar 30, 2012 7:40 pm Quite the contrary. It's gritty yes but also uplifting. It smacks you in the face and tells you to get out of your shell even if you get hurt. Besides, I don't think Mr. Kill 'Em All is in the position to judge other works on such parameters.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Mar 30, 2012 7:47 pm Off the top of my head, Now and Then, Here and There .

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Fri Mar 30, 2012 10:08 pm 1. That's practically the exact opposite of Eva's message 2. Tomino is demonstrably out of his goddamn mind

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Fri Mar 30, 2012 10:25 pm Ornette wrote: Off the top of my head, Now and Then, Here and There .

[]: A demoralized show. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Mar 30, 2012 10:55 pm Ga-Rei Zero . Could hardly go an episode without killing a main character, and that's after killing the entire initial cast in just the first 20 minutes. All killed by, as it turns out, someone we get to see slowly become a bad guy/gal. A Japanese school girl with a sword, honestly, who else could it be? EDIT: Alternatively, I used to think of Ergo Proxy in this way, now I realize it's just boring. Same to Fantastic Children .

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 30, 2012 10:55 pm I can think of far worse examples than NGE, many of them with Tomino's name attached (Ideon, anyone?). And that's without getting into hentai and such, which can get really, really grim. NGE's actually pretty damn positive when you think about it, ambiguous ending notwithstanding.

[]:             Quote Post by bogey » Fri Mar 30, 2012 11:38 pm I don't know how anyone can say with a straight face that NGE was a 'positive' anime

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Mar 30, 2012 11:42 pm The protagonist winds up better off than he was to start with, literally everybody who got in the way of his enlightenment winds up dead, and the one person who didn't survives as well. What's negative about that?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Mar 31, 2012 12:15 am bogey wrote: I don't know how anyone can say with a straight face that NGE was a 'positive' anime

[]: Re: A demoralized show. B/             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sat Mar 31, 2012 12:21 am Alaska Slim wrote: EDIT: Alternatively, I used to think of Ergo Proxy in this way, now I realize it's just boring. Same to Fantastic Children .

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:48 am I think every harem anime is far more mean spirited than Eva. By far. They all regulate the female characters in some down to being trophies who live and worship the very ground the spineless male character the fans wish they could be. I love Evangelion because even though it's animated and features giant monsters, explosions, and biomechanical creatures, the characters are HUMAN and understandable. Turning everyone into a Fuck Object or walking Moe is worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Sat Mar 31, 2012 2:04 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: or walking Moe is worse.

[]: Re: Is Eva The Most Mean-Spirited Anime Of All Time?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 31, 2012 2:55 am I think Tomino was being jealous that NGE went viral in a way none of his creations, even Gundam, quite did. Given that the message was about getting over your depression or if not, then ending up strangling your crush in the ruins of Armageddon, I think that was a complete misreading of the series. If you want mean-spirited, then there's crap like Baccano out there,

[]: Re: Is Eva The Most Mean-Spirited Anime Of All Time?             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Mar 31, 2012 5:34 am bogey wrote: was Tomino right? was eva a series that said its okay to be fucked up and depressed? do you think at its core that eva is a mean-spirited anime?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Mar 31, 2012 11:57 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: I think every harem anime is far more mean spirited than Eva. By far. They all regulate the female characters in some down to being trophies who live and worship the very ground the invertebrate male character the fans wish they could be. I love Evangelion because even though it's animated and features giant monsters, explosions, and biomechanical creatures, the characters are HUMAN and understandable. Turning everyone into a Fuck Object or walking Moe is worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:08 pm What I don't get is why everyone compares NGE to Ideon when they have very little in common. Sure their title mechs have entities that cause berserk power ups, have lots of death at the end, and an underlying theme of species unity; but outside of that they have next to nothing in common outside of being mecha anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:18 pm That's sadly, one of the common misconceptions. EVA's message is quite the opposite, as some have pointed out. The angst it the means, not the ends, it isn't there for its own sake. The author discribes it as "a story where the main character witnesses many horrors with his own eyes, but still tries to stand up again. It is a story of will; a story of moving forward, if only just a little. It is a story of fear, where someone who must face indefinite solitude fears reaching out to others, but still wants to try."

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:58 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: I think every harem anime is far more mean spirited than Eva. By far. They all regulate the female characters in some down to being trophies who live and worship the very ground the invertebrate male character the fans wish they could be.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Fri Mar 30, 2012 7:20 pm That title belongs to Space Warrior Baldios, NG
